definition: Financial conditions indices systematically mismeasure the policy stance under positive correlation, understating tightening by the equivalent of the portfolio vol increase (~40-46%). 60/40 vol rises from ~8.4% (ρ=-0.30) to ~12.3% (ρ=+0.30), triggering deleveraging not captured by standard FCI measures.
evidence: 46% portfolio vol increase arithmetic is uncontested
Triggers VaR-based deleveraging, risk-parity rebalancing, pension ALM pressure
Not captured by Goldman FCI, Chicago Fed NFCI, or Bloomberg FCI
Pair_2 debate upgraded assessment: 'logic is tighter than confidence score reflected'
open_questions: Whether the Fed accounts for this mismeasurement informally
Precise magnitude of mismeasurement vs its existence
